DECLARATION OF BERT CARTER, JR. IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL FILED BY PLAINTIFF THOMPSON LAWSON, LLP
PANEL OF ARBITRATORS ASSIGNED. REJECTION DATE MAR-17-2005.

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND DEMANDS FOR INSPECTION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, FOR AN ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS IS MOOT AS IT SEEKS RESPONSES. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THOSE RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $450, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. LATE OPPOSITION HAS BEEN CONSIDERED. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar May-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion To Compel Responses Without Objection To Form Interrogatories and Demands For Inspection and Production Of Documents, For An Order Establishing Admissions is moot as it seeks responses. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $450, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
